data.description:

RB Global ( (TSE:RBA) ) has shared an announcement.

At its April 30, 2026 annual and special meeting, RB Global shareholders approved setting the board size at ten directors and elected all ten nominees to one-year terms, reinforcing continuity in the company’s leadership and governance structure. Shareholders also reappointed Ernst & Young LLP as independent auditor and backed the non-binding “Say on Pay” executive compensation resolution, signaling overall support for current financial oversight and pay practices.

Investors further passed a special resolution giving the board flexibility to adjust the number of directors within the limits of the company’s governing documents, strengthening the directors’ ability to manage future board composition. A shareholder proposal to require all future shareholder meetings to be held in a hybrid in-person and virtual format was rejected, indicating the company and its investors prefer to retain discretion over how meetings are conducted.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:RBA) stock is a Buy with a C$130.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on RB Global stock, see the TSE:RBA Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Borealis Foods Secures New Oxus Loan, Reshapes Governance
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Borealis Foods ( (BRLS) ).

On April 27, 2026, Borealis Foods’ subsidiaries entered into a $17 million secured term loan with major shareholder and former SPAC sponsor Oxus Capital, using the proceeds to fully repay about $16.2 million owed under a prior Frontwell Capital facility and related costs, freeing the company from Frontwell’s cash controls and associated restructuring requirements. The new loan, carrying 12% interest and a potential interest-for-equity conversion, also ties to a board shake-up adding two Oxus designees, while a separate conversion agreement could automatically turn roughly $33.3 million of shareholder debt into equity if a $70 million equity raise is not completed by July 1, 2026, materially diluting existing holders but easing going-concern pressures and reshaping governance and capital structure amid Nasdaq compliance challenges.

In parallel with the refinancing, Borealis terminated its Chief Restructuring Officer engagement, which had been imposed under the Frontwell forbearance, and secured board approval from disinterested directors for the related-party financing and conversion arrangements. These moves collectively provide longer-term liquidity, reduce immediate refinancing risk, and create a framework for substantial deleveraging via debt-to-equity conversions, but they also deepen Oxus’s influence and introduce potential dilution and control implications for current shareholders.

Spark’s Take on BRLS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BRLS is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by very weak financial performance (large losses, negative equity, and ongoing cash burn) and bearish technical signals (below major moving averages with negative MACD). Additional downside risk comes from recent governance and lender-default related corporate events, while valuation provides limited support due to ongoing losses and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on BRLS stock, click here.

More about Borealis Foods

Borealis Foods Inc., through subsidiaries including Palmetto Gourmet Foods, operates in the food manufacturing sector with production and distribution facilities in Saluda, South Carolina. The company has significant involvement from its former SPAC sponsor Oxus Capital PTE Ltd. and is listed on the Nasdaq Stock Market, where it has recently faced filing-related compliance issues.

Average Trading Volume: 1,684,636

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $35.63M

Marpai ( (MRAI) ) has provided an update.

On February 13, 2026, Marpai, Inc. issued a $410,000 promissory note to Chief Executive Officer Damien Lamendola at a 12% annual interest rate, maturing April 11, 2026, and on March 9, 2026 it issued a second $250,000 note to him on the same terms maturing May 10, 2026. On April 29, 2026, Marpai and Lamendola agreed to extend the maturity dates of both notes, including all outstanding principal and interest, to September 1, 2026 while leaving all other terms unchanged, a move that eases near-term liquidity pressures and slightly lengthens the company’s short-term insider financing horizon.

The extension of these insider notes signals a continued willingness by Marpai’s CEO to support the company’s funding needs while deferring repayment, which may offer short-term financial flexibility but underscores the firm’s reliance on related-party debt. For stakeholders, the revised September 2026 maturities reduce immediate refinancing or repayment risk, potentially stabilizing the company’s cash planning over the next several months.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

FirstCash ( (FCFS) ) has issued an update.

On May 1, 2026, FirstCash Holdings, Inc. closed a privately placed $750 million offering of 6.125% senior unsecured notes due May 1, 2034, issued by its wholly owned subsidiary FirstCash, Inc. The notes, guaranteed by the parent and key domestic subsidiaries, carry semi-annual interest payments starting November 1, 2026, and were sold under Rule 144A and Regulation S to qualified investors.

The new notes include a detailed redemption schedule that allows early repayment with a make-whole premium before May 1, 2029 and step-down call prices thereafter, plus provisions for mandatory repurchase following certain asset sales or change-of-control events. Covenants in the indenture restrict additional indebtedness, dividends, stock repurchases, asset transfers, mergers, and affiliate transactions, signaling a disciplined capital structure framework designed to protect noteholders while providing FirstCash with long-term funding flexibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (FCFS) stock is a Buy with a $252.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FirstCash stock, see the FCFS Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FCFS is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial quality (notably robust free cash flow and solid margins) and a clear upward technical trend. Offsetting factors are balance-sheet leverage volatility across periods and only moderate valuation support (P/E ~19.3 with a low dividend yield).

To see Spark’s full report on FCFS stock, click here.

More about FirstCash

FirstCash Holdings, Inc., through its subsidiary FirstCash, Inc., operates as a specialty finance company focused on pawn and related consumer financial services, primarily serving customers who lack access to traditional banking credit. The company typically funds its operations and growth through a mix of unsecured credit facilities and senior unsecured notes in the capital markets.

data.title: Cantaloupe Redeems Preferred Stock Ahead of 365 Merger
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cantaloupe ( (CTLP) ) has provided an announcement.

On May 1, 2026, Cantaloupe issued a notice that it has elected to redeem all outstanding shares of its Series A Convertible Preferred Stock immediately prior to the expected closing of its merger with 365 Retail Markets on May 8, 2026. The redemption price per preferred share is set at $62.90 as of that date, comprising $11.00 plus $51.90 in accrued and unpaid cumulative dividends, and dividends will cease to accrue after the redemption date.

Holders of the preferred shares may instead choose, at any time prior to the redemption date, to convert their preferred stock and associated accrued dividends into common stock and thereby receive the merger consideration for common shares at closing rather than the cash redemption price. The notice, which is revocable at Cantaloupe’s discretion, outlines that payment of the redemption price requires surrender of certificates and a letter of transmittal, and that any preferred stock still outstanding on the redemption date will be redeemed for cash immediately before the merger closes, altering the capital structure ahead of the transaction’s completion.

The most recent analyst rating on (CTLP) stock is a Hold with a $11.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cantaloupe stock, see the CTLP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CTLP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CTLP is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by improved financial performance—stronger profitability and ROE with modest leverage—tempered by uneven cash conversion and historical volatility. Technicals are neutral-to-soft and do not add momentum support, while valuation appears moderate on the provided P/E. Corporate events are mildly supportive but not a major fundamental catalyst.

To see Spark’s full report on CTLP stock, click here.

More about Cantaloupe

Cantaloupe, Inc., a Pennsylvania corporation, has issued Series A Convertible Preferred Stock and common stock and is party to an Agreement and Plan of Merger dated June 15, 2025 with 365 Retail Markets, LLC. Under that deal, Cantaloupe will merge with a Catalyst MergerSub and survive as a wholly owned, indirect subsidiary of 365 Retail Markets, aligning preferred and common equity with the pending transaction.

The latest announcement is out from FibroBiologics, Inc. ( (FBLG) ).

On May 1, 2026, FibroBiologics, Inc. entered into an at-the-market offering agreement with H.C. Wainwright & Co. that allows the company to issue and sell up to $6.15 million of its common stock from time to time through the sales agent. Under the arrangement, FibroBiologics will pay a 3.0% commission on gross proceeds plus specified legal and due diligence reimbursements, may also sell shares to the agent as principal, and expects to use any net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, with no obligation to sell any shares and the offering terminating once the cap is reached or the agreement is ended.

Shares sold under the agreement, if any, will be issued pursuant to FibroBiologics’ shelf registration statement on Form S-3 that was filed on February 3, 2025 and declared effective on February 10, 2025, supported by a base prospectus and a dedicated ATM prospectus supplement. The deal includes customary indemnification and contribution provisions for the sales agent and its affiliates and underscores the company’s strategy of maintaining flexible, market-based access to equity capital while signaling potential dilution and execution risk for existing shareholders, given that actual sales volumes, prices and timing remain uncertain.

Cisco Systems ( (CSCO) ) has shared an announcement.

Cisco Systems announced that M. Victoria Wong will retire as Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, effective May 19, 2026, after which she will serve as an Executive Advisor until July 25, 2026. The company’s Board appointed current Vice President and Corporate Controller Nichlas A. Fink to succeed her as Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, effective May 20, 2026, in a transition that underscores Cisco’s reliance on internal talent development and continuity in its finance leadership.

Fink, 46, joined Cisco in 2016 and has held progressively senior finance roles, most recently serving as Vice President and Corporate Controller since November 2022, with prior experience at Pyxus International, Nortel Networks, and Ernst & Young. In connection with his promotion, he is expected to enter into Cisco’s standard indemnity agreement for senior executives and receive a restricted stock unit award valued at $500,000, aligning his compensation and legal protections with the company’s established framework for top officers.

M&T Bank ( (MTB) ) has issued an update.

On May 1, 2026, M&T Bank Corporation posted a second-quarter 2026 investor presentation outlining its strategic priorities and highlighting its strong financial performance in 2025, which it sees as the foundation for 2026. The bank reported record 2025 net operating income of $2.9 billion and net operating EPS of $17.20, a top-quartile 3.67% net interest margin while growing loans 6% year over year, and improved asset quality with double-digit percentage declines in nonaccrual and criticized loans.

The presentation underscores two enterprise priorities for 2026: “Teaming for Growth,” focused on integrated regional growth, particularly in New England, and “Operational Excellence,” aimed at scalable infrastructure, faster processes and improved customer satisfaction. For stakeholders, M&T points to its 2025 capital returns, including an 11% increase in its quarterly dividend and the repurchase of 9% of outstanding shares, and emphasizes its competitive standing versus peers on metrics such as return on tangible assets, efficiency ratio and pre-provision net revenue relative to risk-weighted assets.

The most recent analyst rating on (MTB) stock is a Hold with a $227.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on M&T Bank stock, see the MTB Stock Forecast page.

MTB scores well on solid underlying financial performance and a constructive technical trend (price above key moving averages with positive MACD). Valuation adds support via a moderate P/E and dividend yield, while the earnings call was balanced—strong asset quality, fee momentum, and capital returns offset by softer quarter-over-quarter earnings, modest NII pressure, and higher operating expenses.
